Argument.Name: PROJECT_NAME
Argument.Description: The name of the project to initialize

Option.Description: Enable agentic features and AI automation
Docker build argument in KEY=VALUE format (can be specified multiple times)
Write devcontainer build output to a log file instead of console
Optional project description
Force overwrite existing files
Path to a local template directory (bypasses NuGet for testing)
Enable Model Context Protocol (MCP) integration
Skip the prompt to spawn devcontainer
Don't automatically launch VS Code
Custom NuGet source/feed to search for templates
Include prerelease/preview template packages
Interactively prompt for Docker build arguments with defaults from devcontainer.json
Automatically spawn devcontainer after initialization
SSH target for remote spawn (user@host or host from registered targets)
NuGet tag to filter templates (default: pks-templates)
Template short name to use (e.g., pks-claude-dotnet9)
Custom volume name for devcontainer
Use AI to optimize deployment strategy
Target environment (dev, staging, prod)
Number of replicas to deploy
Watch deployment progress in real-time
Include AI-powered insights and recommendations
Environment to check (dev, staging, prod, all)
Watch status in real-time
Action to perform (create, list, status, start, stop, remove)
Path to agent configuration file
Agent ID for status, start, stop, or remove operations
Name of the agent
Additional settings in key=value format
Type of agent (automation, monitoring, deployment, etc.)

Argument.Name: TEXT
Argument.Description: Text to convert to ASCII art

Option.Description: Animate the ASCII art
Color for the ASCII art
Apply gradient coloring
ASCII art style (banner, block, digital, starwars)

Argument.Name: MESSAGE
Argument.Description: The message or feedback to include in the report

Option.Description: Report a bug (adds bug label)
Preview the report without creating the GitHub issue
Request a feature (adds enhancement label)
Include environment and system information
Include anonymous telemetry data in the report
Include PKS CLI version information
Ask a question (adds question label)
Target repository (default: pksorensen/pks-cli)
Title for the GitHub issue
Delay in milliseconds for performance demo
Enable interactive prompts
Simulate an error for demonstration
User ID for logging (optional)
Enable verbose output
Path to configuration file
Enable debug mode with verbose logging
Port number for HTTP/SSE transport
Transport mode (stdio, http, sse)
Execute a test swarm tool to verify functionality
Show what would be done without making changes
Force overwrite existing files
Output directory path for devcontainer files
Enable verbose output

Argument.Name: NAME
Argument.Description: Name of the devcontainer configuration

Option.Description: Use Docker Compose instead of single container
Environment variables in KEY=VALUE format
Comma-separated list of VS Code extensions to include
Comma-separated list of features to include
Additional files to generate (gitignore,vscode,readme)
Enable Git credential sharing
Base container image to use
Include development packages and tools
Run in interactive mode with prompts
Comma-separated list of ports to forward
Command to run after container creation
Template to use for initialization
Workspace folder path inside container
Comma-separated list of additional NuGet sources to include
Enable debug output for troubleshooting template discovery
Enable expert mode with advanced options
Discover and use templates from NuGet packages
Use quick setup with minimal prompts
Skip extension selection step
Skip feature selection step
Skip template selection step
Comma-separated list of NuGet sources to use for template discovery

Argument.Name: CONFIG-PATH
Argument.Description: Path to devcontainer.json file to validate

Option.Description: Validate VS Code extensions
Validate feature configurations
Enable strict validation mode

Argument.Name: PROJECT_PATH
Argument.Description: Path to project directory (defaults to current directory)

Option.Description: Automatically rebuild without prompting when configuration changes detected
Path to Docker config.json to forward (defaults to ~/.docker/config.json)
Forward Docker credentials from host to devcontainer (default: true, matches VS Code behavior)
Use direct execution instead of bootstrap container (advanced)
Don't copy source files (only .devcontainer configuration)
Disable Docker credential forwarding (use when you don't want host Docker credentials in container)
Don't automatically launch VS Code after spawning
Skip rebuild even if configuration changes detected
Force rebuild even if no configuration changes detected
Custom volume name for the devcontainer
Show all containers (not just running)
Output format: table or json (default: table)

Argument.Name: CONTAINER_ID
Argument.Description: Container ID to connect to (optional - will prompt if not provided)

Option.Description: Don't automatically launch VS Code after connecting
Connect to a devcontainer on a remote SSH target (host, label, or user@host)

Argument.Name: CONTAINER_ID
Argument.Description: Container ID to destroy (optional - will prompt if not provided)

Option.Description: Enable verbose output

Argument.Name: OWNER_PROJECT
Argument.Description: Owner/project in owner/project format

Option.Description: Runner name (defaults to hostname)
Agentics server URL (falls back to AGENTIC_SERVER env, then agentics.dk)
Git user.email to configure inside the devcontainer (default: si-14x@agentics.dk)
Git user.name to configure inside the devcontainer (default: si-14x)
Execute jobs in-process instead of spawning devcontainers (for testing)
Polling interval in seconds (default: 10)
Project to run for in owner/project format. Auto-registers if not already registered. Without this, uses first saved registration.
Agentics server URL (falls back to AGENTIC_SERVER env, then agentics.dk). Used when auto-registering with --project.
Path to vibecast binary (default: uses VIBECAST_BINARY env or 'npx vibecast')
Base work directory (default: .agentics/_work)
Enable verbose output
Full URL to the assembly line (e.g. https://agentics.dk/p/owner/project/assembly-lines/stage-id)
Target column ID (defaults to the first column in the stage)
Task description or raw error output
Comma-separated labels to apply to the task
Task priority: low, medium, high (default: high)
Override ALP server URL (default: from runner registration)
Task title (required)
Enable verbose output
Enable verbose output

Argument.Name: REPO
Argument.Description: Repository in owner/repo format

Option.Description: Comma-separated runner labels (default: devcontainer-runner)

Argument.Name: REPO
Argument.Description: Repository in owner/repo format

Argument.Name: REPO
Argument.Description: Repository in owner/repo format

Argument.Name: REPO
Argument.Description: Repository in owner/repo format

Option.Description: Maximum number of concurrent jobs (default: 1)

Argument.Name: REPO
Argument.Description: Repository in owner/repo format

Argument.Name: REPO
Argument.Description: Repository in owner/repo format

Argument.Name: URL
Argument.Description: Coolify instance URL (e.g. https://projects.si14agents.com)

Option.Description: Dump raw API responses for debugging
Show verbose output
API token (will prompt if not provided)
Dump raw API responses for debugging
Show verbose output
Dump raw API responses for debugging
Show verbose output

Argument.Name: TARGET
Argument.Description: SSH target in user@host format (e.g., root@myserver.com)

Option.Description: Path to SSH private key file
Friendly label for this target
SSH port (default: 22)
Test SSH connectivity after registering

Argument.Name: TARGET
Argument.Description: SSH target to remove (host, label, or user@host)

Argument.Name: hostname
Argument.Description: Registry hostname (e.g. registry.kjeldager.io)

Option.Description: Enable verbose output
Force re-authentication even if already authenticated
Enable verbose output
Show HTTP request/response details for troubleshooting
Force re-authentication even if already authenticated
Enable verbose output

Argument.Name: url
Argument.Description: Jira URL with JQL query

Option.Description: Show HTTP request/response details for troubleshooting
Custom JQL query
Name for the saved filter
Output directory for exported markdown files (default: .jira)
Project key to browse directly
Save the JQL filter for future use
Enable verbose output
Set the acceptance criteria custom field ID (e.g. customfield_10064)
Show HTTP request/response details for troubleshooting
Show current field mappings
Enable verbose output
Enable verbose output
Force re-authentication even if already authenticated
Azure AD tenant ID (defaults to 'common')
Enable verbose output
Output as JSON including token, endpoint, and default model
Token scope (defaults to cognitive services)
Enable verbose output
Re-register even if a key is already stored

Argument.Name: prompt
Argument.Description: The image generation prompt

Option.Description: Aspect ratio: 1:1, 3:4, 4:3, 9:16, 16:9, auto (default: auto)
Input image to augment or edit (path to jpg/png). When set, prompt becomes an editing instruction.
List available image generation models and exit
Model to use (default: gemini-3.1-flash-image-preview)
Output file path (default: ./image-<timestamp>.jpg)
Path to a text file containing the prompt
Image generation provider (default: google)
Output resolution, e.g. 512, 1024, 2048, 4096 (default: model decides)

Argument.Name: prompt
Argument.Description: The credential prompt from Git

Option.Description: Install the GIT_ASKPASS wrapper script and configure your shell
Force overwrite existing hooks configuration
Output result in JSON format (suppresses banner and UI output)
Settings scope: user (global), project (current directory), or local (.claude folder)
Force overwrite existing hooks configuration
Output result in JSON format (suppresses banner and UI output)
Settings scope: user (global), project (current directory), or local (.claude folder)

Force overwrite existing hooks configuration

Settings scope: user (global), project (current directory), or local (.claude folder)
Path to PRD configuration file
Disable colored output
Output format: markdown, json
Enable verbose output with detailed information

Argument.Name: IDEA_DESCRIPTION
Argument.Description: Description of the idea or project to generate PRD for

Option.Description: Business context or background for the project
Overwrite existing PRD file if it exists
Interactive mode for detailed PRD generation
Name of the project (if different from current directory)
Output path for the generated PRD file
Comma-separated list of stakeholders
Target audience for the project
Comma-separated list of technical constraints
PRD template type (standard, technical, mobile, web, api, minimal, enterprise)

Argument.Name: FILE_PATH
Argument.Description: Path to the PRD file to load

Option.Description: Export loaded PRD to different format
Display PRD metadata and statistics
Validate the PRD after loading

Argument.Name: FILE_PATH
Argument.Description: Path to the PRD file (defaults to ./docs/PRD.md)

Option.Description: Filter by assignee
Export requirements to file (CSV, JSON)
Filter by priority (critical, high, medium, low, nice)
Show detailed requirement information
Filter by requirement status (draft, approved, inprogress, completed, blocked, cancelled, onhold)
Filter by requirement type (functional, nonfunctional, business, technical, security, performance, usability, accessibility, compliance)

Argument.Name: FILE_PATH
Argument.Description: Path to the PRD file (defaults to ./docs/PRD.md)

Option.Description: Check status of all PRD files in the project
Export status report to file
Include change history in status report
Watch for changes and update status in real-time

Argument.Name: FILE_PATH
Argument.Description: Path to the PRD file (defaults to ./docs/PRD.md)

Option.Description: Attempt to automatically fix validation issues
Generate validation report file
Use strict validation rules

Argument.Name: PROJECT_NAME
Argument.Description: Name of the project for template generation

Option.Description: List available template types
Output path for the template file
Template type (standard, technical, mobile, web, api, minimal, enterprise)
